migrationTimeline:
2030 | High-risk use cases migrated
2035 | Full migration of all systems complete
processParticipation: NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap
provenance: EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one.
Model 2026-06-14 (EU / taker, High): EU coordinated roadmap (NIS Cooperation Group); adopts the NIST baseline as a taker. No national algorithm.
classificationBasis: EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one.
mainRegulation:
NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555, Art. 21(2)(h) | EU | binding-law
DORA, Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 (financial sector) | EU | binding-law
Commission Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 on a coordinated PQC roadmap | EU | soft-law
NIS Cooperation Group Coordinated Implementation Roadmap (2026/2030/2035) | EU | soft-law
obligation: Essential and important entities must use state-of-the-art cryptography under NIS2, transposed into national law, and financial entities face equivalent duties under DORA; neither yet names post-quantum algorithms specifically. Migration follows the EU coordinated roadmap, with high-risk use cases targeted for 2030 and full migration by 2035, which is encouraged rather than mandated.
summary: Austria migrates within the EU coordinated roadmap as a member state. No national post-quantum-specific instrument or authority guidance is yet on record, and transposition of the NIS2 Directive, which is expected to absorb post-quantum requirements over time, was still pending in mid-2026. Austria has invested substantially in quantum technology more broadly, but not in a dedicated post-quantum migration policy.
